title: SNARE-seq2
author: Nongluk Plongthongkum
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bzdsp26e

steps.title: 40% (wt/vol) PEG 6000
steps.instruction: 40% (wt/vol) PEG 6000. Weigh 16.0 g of PEG 6000 in 50 mL tube. Add nuclease-free water and bring the total volume to 40 mL. Rotate the tube at room temperature until PEG 6000 completely dissolved. Spin down the tube at 200 g for 2 min, at room temperature to remove the tiny bubble. CRITICAL: 40% (wt/vol) of PEG 6000 is very viscous and difficult to filter through a 0.22 µm filter. We suggest preparing 40% PEG freshly before making GLR buffer. When PEG is diluted in 4x GLR buffer, it is easier to filter.

steps.title: 4x GLR buffer
steps.instruction: 4x GLR buffer. To prepare 40 mL of 4x GLR buffer, Add 2.64 mL of nuclease-free water, 10.56 mL of 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 0.8 mL of 1 M MgCl2 and 4 mL of 2 M KCl into 50 mL tube. Gently mix well by vortexing. Add 22 mL of 40% (wt/vol) PEG 6000 and gently mix well by vortexing. Filter through 0.22 µm into a new 50 mL tube and briefly spin the tube at room temperature for 30 s. Aliquot 1.8 mL into 2 mL tubes to minimize contamination from each use and store at 4 ºC.

steps.title: 10% (vol/vol) Triton X-100
steps.instruction: 10% (vol/vol) Triton X-100. Slowly aspirate 2.0 mL of Triton X-100 liquid with low retention pipette tip and slowly dispend into 18.0 mL nuclease-free water in 50 mL tube. Dissolve Triton X-100 by slowly rotate the tube until the solution is clear. Filter 10% Triton X-100 solution through 0.22 µm syringe filter into a new 50 mL tube and store at room temperature. CRITICAL: If it’s difficult to pipette Triton X-100 accurately as it’s a viscous liquid, we may warm it at 37 ºC before pipetting

steps.title: 10% (vol/vol) Tween 20
steps.instruction: 10% (vol/vol) Tween 20. Tween 20 is very viscous liquid and difficult to pipette accurately. We convert the volume in cm3 into grams using the density of tween 20 at 25 ºC is 1.1 cm3. To prepare 20 mL of 10% (vol/vol) Tween 20, weigh 2.2 g of Tween 20 in 50 mL tube. Add 18.0 mL of nuclease-free water and invert or rotate the tube slowly at room temperature until Tween 20 is completely dissolved in water. Filter 10% Tween 20 solution through 0.22 µm syringe filter into a new 50 mL tube and store at room temperature.

steps.title: 25 mM dNTP mix
steps.instruction: 25 mM dNTP mix. Mix 250 µL each of 100 mM dATP, dCTP, dGTP and dTTP in 1.5 mL tube. Mix well by vortexing and briefly spin the tube at room temperature for 5 s. Aliquot 250 µL into each of 1.5 mL tube and store at -20 ºC for a couple of years

steps.title: 2x Lysis buffer
steps.instruction: 2x Lysis buffer. To prepare 25 mL of 2x Lysis buffer, add 6.5 mL of nuclease-free water into 50 mL tube. Add 0.5 mL of 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 2 mL of 5 M NaCl, 5 mL of 0.5 M EDTA and 11 mL of 10% (wt/vol) SDS. Gently mix and aliquot 1.8 mL into 2 mL tubes and store at room temperature.

steps.title: 1x B&W-T buffer
steps.instruction: 1x B&W-T buffer. To prepare 40 mL of 1x B&W-T, add 31.56 mL of nuclease-free water into 50 mL tube. Add 200 µL of 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 8 mL of 5 M NaCl, 40 µL of 0.5 M EDTA and 200 µL of 10% (vol/vol) Tween 20. Gently mix by vortexing and store at room temperature.

steps.title: 2x B&W buffer
steps.instruction: 2x B&W buffer. To prepare 25 mL of 1x B&W, add 14.7 mL of nuclease-free water into 50 mL tube. Add 250 µL of 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 10 mL of 5 M NaCl, and 50 µL of 0.5 M EDTA. Mix well by vortexing and store at room temperature.

0.1 M PMSF. Weigh 34.8 mg of PMSF and transfer into 2 mL microtube. Add 100% isopropanol to 2 mL and vortex vigorously to dissolve PMSF. Quick spin the tube down and transfer all solution into 3 mL syringe. Filter through a 0.22 µm syringe filter to a new 2 mL tube. Aliquot 50 µL per 0.2 mL PCR tube and store at -20 ºC for up to 4 months. CRITICAL: To maintain the activity of PMSF in solution, store PMSF in single-use aliquots.

steps.title: Transposon preparation
steps.instruction: Transposon preparation. Resuspend Nextera adapter 1, 5P-Nextera adapter 2 and mosaic end (ME) oligos with nuclease-free water to 100 µM. Mix 500 µL of 100 µM Nextera adapter 1 and 500 µL of 100 µM ME in 1.5 mL DNA LoBind microtube, aliquot 30 µL of non-annealed transposons into each of 0.2 mL PCR tube and store at -20 ºC for up to 1-2 years. Prepare 5P-Nextera adapter 2 and ME the same way as Nextera adapter 1. All sequences of oligos can be found in the attached tables.

steps.title: Accessible chromatin (AC) Round 1 barcoded oligos preparation (Plate A)
steps.instruction: Accessible chromatin (AC) Round 1 barcoded oligos preparation (Plate A). Prepare 50 µL of 25 µM accessible chromatin (AC) Round 1 barcoded oligos annealed with 23 µM accessible chromatin (AC) Round 1 linker oligos (BC_0100)

steps.title: Resuspend AC Round 1 linker (BC_0100)...
steps.instruction: Resuspend AC Round 1 linker (BC_0100) with nuclease-free water to final concentration of 1 mM

steps.title: Prepare 2
steps.instruction: Prepare 2.5 mL of 30.67 µM AC Round 1 linker by adding 76.68 µL of 1 mM of AC Round 1 linker to 2,423.32 µL of nuclease-free water in 5 mL tube and mix well by vortexing

steps.title: Add 12
steps.instruction: Add 12.5 µL of 100 µM AC Round 1 barcoded oligos into 96-well plate (total 48 wells, rows A - D) with multichannel pipette

steps.title: Transfer AC Round 1 linker oligos into 25 mL reservoir
steps.instruction: Transfer AC Round 1 linker oligos into 25 mL reservoir

steps.title: Add 37
steps.instruction: Add 37.5 µL of 30.67 µM AC Round 1 linker into each well of AC Round 1 barcodes with multichannel pipette and mix well by pipetting 12 times (mixing volume 45 µL)

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP: Seal and spin down the plate on 96-well plate swinging bucket rotor at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC.

steps.title: Anneal AC Round 1 barcoded oligos...
steps.instruction: Anneal AC Round 1 barcoded oligos and AC linker oligos on thermocycler using the following annealing program: 95 ºC for 2 min, slowly cool down to 20 ºC (0.1 ºC/s) and hold at 4 ºC. CRITICAL STEP: After annealing oligonucleotides in the plate, we recommend spinning down the plate and use a sterile needle to punch the holes on the sealing film to release the pressure in every single well. Otherwise, the liquid inside the well will be pulled up to the top of the well when the film is unsealed, and this can lead to barcode cross-contamination. PAUSE POINT: If do not want to continue to mix these annealed oligos in the next step, store that plate at -20 ºC

steps.title: RNA reverse transcription (RT) Round 1...
steps.instruction: RNA reverse transcription (RT) Round 1 barcoded oligos preparation (Plate B). Prepare 50 µL of 25 µM of oligo (dT)15 and 25 µM of random hexamer (N6) RT barcoded oligos (see attached tables) mix in each of 48 wells. CRITICAL STEP: 100 µM of dT and N6 reverse transcription barcoded oligos are ordered in row A-D and row E-H of oligo plate, respectively.

steps.title: Transfer 12
steps.instruction: Transfer 12.5 µL of 100 µM of rows A-D (dT) in the RT barcoded oligos plate to rows A-D of a new 96-well DNA LoBind plate. CRITICAL STEP If use electronic multichannel pipette, add 1 µL of air after aspirating to avoid cross-contamination of barcoded oligos. This can be applied to other steps when we have to transfer barcoded oligos from stock plate to a new plate. Make sure that oligos are delivered to the bottom of the well.

steps.title: Transfer 12
steps.instruction: Transfer 12.5 µL of 100 µM of rows E-H oligos (N6) in RT barcoded oligos plate to rows A-D (row E to row A, row F to row B, row G to row C, row H to row D) of 96-well plate that contains dT barcoded oligos above

steps.title: Pipette 2 mL of nuclease-free water into 25 mL reservoir
steps.instruction: Pipette 2 mL of nuclease-free water into 25 mL reservoir

steps.title: Add 25 µL of nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL of nuclease-free water to row A-D of RNA Round 1 stock plate and mix well by pipetting 12 times (mixing volume 45 µL)

steps.title: Seal and spin down the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and spin down the plate at 160 g for 1 min, 4 ºC and leave the plate on ice or PCR cool rack

steps.title: AC Round 1 barcoded oligos and...
steps.instruction: AC Round 1 barcoded oligos and RT Round 1 barcoded oligos mix. The final concentration of each oligo (dT, N6, AC) in the oligo mix is 12.5 µM.

steps.title: Transfer 50 µL of oligos in...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µL of oligos in plate B (RT Round 1 barcoded oligos) into plate A that contain 50 µL of annealed AC Round 1 barcoded oligos and linker at identical well IDs (rows A-D) and mix well by pipetting 12 times (mixing volume 90 µL)

steps.title: Spin down the plate at 160...
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate at 160 g for 1 min, 4 ºC and put the plate on PCR cool rack

steps.title: Aliquot 4 µL of mixed Round...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 4 µL of mixed Round 1 barcoded oligos (rows A-D) into 10-25 of 96-well plates as “working plates”

steps.title: Spin down working plates at 160...
steps.instruction: Spin down working plates at 160 g for 1 min, 4 ºC and store at -20 ºC for up to a couple of years. Store the left over Round 1 stock plate at -20 ºC.

steps.title: Ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos
steps.instruction: Ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos. Prepare stock plate of 100 µL of 18 µM ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos annealed with 16.5 µM ligation Round 2 linker (BC_0215).

steps.title: Resuspend ligation Round 2 linker (BC_0215)...
steps.instruction: Resuspend ligation Round 2 linker (BC_0215) with nuclease-free water to final concentration of 1 mM

steps.title: Prepare 9 mL of 20
steps.instruction: Prepare 9 mL of 20.12 µM ligation Round 2 linker by adding 181.08 µL of 1 mM round 2 linker to 8,818.9 µL of nuclease-free water in 15 mL tube and mix well by vortexing

steps.title: Add 18 µL of 100 µM...
steps.instruction: Add 18 µL of 100 µM of ligation round 2 barcoded oligos into 96-well plate (rows A-H)

steps.title: Transfer ligation Round 2 linker into 25 mL reservoir
steps.instruction: Transfer ligation Round 2 linker into 25 mL reservoir

steps.title: Add 82 µL of 20
steps.instruction: Add 82 µL of 20.12 µM ligation Round 2 linker to each well of ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos with and mix well by pipetting 10 times (mixing volume 90 µL)

steps.title: Seal and spin down the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and spin down the plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC

steps.title: Anneal ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos...
steps.instruction: Anneal ligation Round 2 barcoded oligos and Round 2 linker on thermocycler using the annealing program for Round 1 barcoded oligos and keep on ice

steps.title: Spin down the plate at 160...
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC and keep on ice

steps.title: Aliquot 10 µL of annealed ligation...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 10 µL of annealed ligation Round 2 barcoded/linker oligos into 10 of 96-well plate as “working plate”

steps.title: Spin down the working plate at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the working plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC before store at -20ºC for up to a couple of year

steps.title: Ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos
steps.instruction: Ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos. Prepare stock plate of 100 µL of 21 µM Round 3 barcoded oligos annealed with 19.5 µM ligation Round 3 linker (BC_0060).

steps.title: Resuspend ligation Round 3 linker (BC_0060)...
steps.instruction: Resuspend ligation Round 3 linker (BC_0060) with nuclease-free water to final concentration of 1 mM

steps.title: Prepare 8
steps.instruction: Prepare 8.5 mL of 24.68 µM ligation Round 3 linker by adding 209.8 µL of 1 mM Round 3 linker to 8,290.2 µL of nuclease-free water in 15 mL tube and mix well by vortexing

steps.title: Add 21 µL of 100 µM...
steps.instruction: Add 21 µL of 100 µM of ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos into 96-well plate (rows A-H)

steps.title: Transfer ligation Round 3 linker into 25 mL reservoir
steps.instruction: Transfer ligation Round 3 linker into 25 mL reservoir

steps.title: Add 79 µL of 24
steps.instruction: Add 79 µL of 24.68 µM ligation Round 3 linker to each well of ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos and mix well by pipetting 12 times (mixing volume 90 µL)

steps.title: Seal and spin down the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and spin down the plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC

steps.title: Anneal ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos...
steps.instruction: Anneal ligation Round 3 barcoded oligos and Round 3 linker on thermocycler using the annealing program for Round 1 barcoded oligos and keep on ice

steps.title: Spin down the plate at 160...
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC and keep on ice

steps.title: Aliquot 10 µL of annealed ligation...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 10 µL of annealed ligation Round 3 barcoded/linker oligos into 10 of 96-well plate as “working plate”

steps.title: Spin down the working plate at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the working plate at 160 g for 1min, 4 ºC before store at -20ºC for up to a couple of year

steps.title: Convert Tn5 transposase (Diagenode Tagmentase, Diagenode...
steps.instruction: Convert Tn5 transposase (Diagenode Tagmentase, Diagenode #C01070010-20, EZ-Tn5 Transposase, Lucigen #TNP92110, or expressed/purified with an in house protocol). For example, if the concentration of unloaded Tn5 is 0.40 mg/mL, the monomer concentration in uM is 7.55 µM based on molar mass of monomer Tn5 ~53,000 g/mol [0.4 mg/mL * (1 mol/53,000 g)]. CRITICAL STEP: Anneal transposons and load Tn5 on the day of experiment 1-2 h prior experiment starts. Combine transposons and mix well before adding Tn5 to make sure that both transposons are sufficiently homogeneous prior to mixing with Tn5. Avoid generating bubbles when mixing Tn5 with transposons by pipetting slowly and do not fill pipette tip with air. If the experiment is not ready, store loaded Tn5 at -20 ºC.

steps.title: Thaw non-annealed transposons (Nextera adapter 1/ME...
steps.instruction: Thaw non-annealed transposons (Nextera adapter 1/ME and 5P-Nextera adapter 2/ME) on ice. Briefly vortex and quick spin the tube. Anneal transposons on thermocycler using following program: 95 ºC 5 min, slowly cool down to 65 ºC (0.1 ºC/s), 65 ºC 5 min, slowly cool down to 4 ºC (0.1 ºC/s) and hold at 4 ºC.

steps.title: Load transposons 1
steps.instruction: Load transposons 1.5x molar ratio to Tn5. The amount below is sufficient for tagmenting ~ 1.8 million nuclei/cells. 13 µL, 37.72 uM monomer Tagmentase (2mg/mL) 7.35 µL, 50 uM Annealed Nextera adapter 1/ME 7.35 µL, 50 uM Annealed 5P-Nextera adapter 2/ME 39 µL, Tagmentase Dilution Buffer Add annealed Nextera adapter 1/ME and annealed 5P-Nextera adapter 2/ME into the bottom of 1.5 mL DNA LoBind microtube, mix well by pipetting 10 times or gently vortexing and briefly spin the tube on mini centrifuge for 3 s. Addunloaded Tn5 and mix by gently pipetting 20 times (set the volume of p200 pipette to 80 µL). Quick spin the tube and incubate at 25 ºC for 0.5 h, 350 rpm. The final concentration of loaded Tn5 is 7.35 µM (monomer Tn5 concentration). 00:30:00

steps.title: Isolate nuclei from tissue following tissue-specific...
steps.instruction: Isolate nuclei from tissue following tissue-specific nuclei extraction protocol ( dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ufketkw ) with 0.1 U/µL of SUPERase In RNase Inhibitor and 0.2 U/µL of Enzymatics RNase Inhibitor. For cell lines, nuclei can be extracted with ATAC Lysis buffer with 0.1% NP-40 as previously described with the addition of RNase inhibitors, and increase lysis volume proportional to the number of input cells.

steps.title: Prepare 1x PBS + RI (1...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1x PBS + RI (1 mL per sample) and keep on ice. 1000 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 2.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 1.25 µL, Enzymatics Rnase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: Prepare 1% (wt/vol) formaldehyde in 1x...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1% (wt/vol) formaldehyde in 1x PBS (1 mL per sample) and keep on ice. CRITICAL: formaldehyde solution should be in 1x PBS and methanol free. 937.5 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 62.5 µL, Formaldehyde, 16% wt/vol

steps.title: Resuspend 1-2 million nuclei with 1...
steps.instruction: Resuspend 1-2 million nuclei with 1 mL 1x PBS + RI and keep on ice.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 1% formaldehyde...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 1% formaldehyde to nuclei suspension and pipette gently 8 times. Leave the tube on ice for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Fixation

steps.title: Pellet nuclei at 900 g for...
steps.instruction: Pellet nuclei at 900 g for 8 min at 4C using bucket rotor centrifuge.

steps.title: Prepare 1x PBS + 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 1x PBS + 0.1% (wt/vol) BSA + RI ( 1 mL per sample) and leave on ice. 1000 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 13.4 µL, BSA, 7.5% wt/vol 1.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 0.75 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: Prepare 1x Tango Buffer + RI...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1x Tango Buffer + RI (1 mL per ~ 3 million nuclei/cells) and leave on ice. 100 µL, Tango Buffer, 10x 160 µL, DMF, 100% 5 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL) 2.5 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40 U/uL) 732.5 µL, Nuclease-free water

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend pelleted...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend pelleted nuclei with 1 mL of 1x PBS + 0.1% BSA + RI to wash.

steps.title: Pellet nuclei at 900 g for...
steps.instruction: Pellet nuclei at 900 g for 8 min at 4C using bucket rotor centrifuge.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend with...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend with 1x Tango Buffer + RI to have a minimum concentration of 3,400 nuclei per microliter.

steps.title: Count the nuclei using cell counter...
steps.instruction: Count the nuclei using cell counter and resuspend the nuclei solution with additional 1x Tango Buffer + RI to obtain 3,400 nuclei per microliter.

steps.title: Set up tagmentation mix per reaction...
steps.instruction: Set up tagmentation mix per reaction as follows (1 reaction per 150,000 nuclei/cells). Minimum 4 reactions for 1 sample per plate (48 wells of round 1). 3 µL, Tango Buffer, 10x 4.8 µL, DMF, 100% 4.95 µL, Loaded Tn5, 7.35uM 1.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 0.75 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40 U/uL) 15 µL, Nuclease-free water

steps.title: Prepare 150,000 nuclei in 45 µL...
steps.instruction: Prepare 150,000 nuclei in 45 µL of 1x Tango Buffer and mix with 30 µL of tagmentation mix. The final concentration of Tn5 and DMF in final reaction is 0.8 µM and 16% (vol/vol), respectively. The ratio of nuclei suspension and tagmentation mix is 3 : 2 or 45 µL : 30 µL. Add nuclei into 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube then add tagmentation mix and mix gently by pipetting 10 times. Briefly spin the tube on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 3 s and aliquot 75 µL of tagmentation reaction into 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP: Set up 4 tubes of tagmentation reactions to have enough nuclei for Round 1 barcoding (8,000 nuclei/well x total 48 wells = 384,000 nuclei). Set up the reactions on ice. Do not incubate tagmentation reaction in large volume to make sure that nuclei are distributed evenly in the reaction not sitting on the bottom of the tube when incubating during tagmentation.

steps.title: Place the tubes on thermomixer and...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on thermomixer and incubate at 37 ºC for 30 min, 300 rpm. 00:30:00, Tagmentation

steps.title: Before incubation is complete, prepare 1x PBS + 0
steps.instruction: Before incubation is complete, prepare 1x PBS + 0.1% (wt/vol) BSA + RI (1 mL per 400 µL tagmented nuclei) and keep on ice 1000 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 13.33 µL, BSA, 7.5% wt/vol 1.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 0.75 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: Remove the tubes from thermomixer and place on ice
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from thermomixer and place on ice. Then pool tagmented nuclei of the same sample into the same tube.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5x volume of 1x PBS + 0.1% (wt/vol) BSA + RI (1000 µL to 400 µL tagmented nuclei) to pooled tagmented nuclei and mix by pipetting gently 5 times and centrifuge at 900 g for 8 min, 4 ºC with swinging bucket rotor

steps.title: During centrifugation, prepare 0
steps.instruction: During centrifugation, prepare 0.5x PBS + RI (1 mL per 1 million nuclei/cells) and keep on ice 500 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 500 µL, Nuclease free water 2.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 1.25 µL, Enzymatics Rnase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP Aspirate supernatant and resuspend...
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP Aspirate supernatant and resuspend nuclei with 300 µL of 0.5x PBS + RI to have a minimum concentration of nuclei not lower than 1,000 nuclei/µL. If different numbers of nuclei in tagmentation are used, adjust suspension volume. Pipetette gently to resuspend

steps.title: Count nuclei concentration with cell counter...
steps.instruction: Count nuclei concentration with cell counter and dilute nuclei to 1,000 nuclei/µL with 0.5x PBS + RI.

steps.title: Thaw Round 1 AC/RT oligo working...
steps.instruction: Thaw Round 1 AC/RT oligo working plate on ice and spin the plate on swinging bucket at 200 g for 1 min, 4 ºC and leave the plate on ice. Note: Can leave in 4C at the beginning of Day 1 to thaw.

steps.title: Prepare GLR-A mix following table below. CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: Prepare GLR-A mix following table below. CRITICAL STEP: Prepare GLR-A mix during washing tagmented nuclei, but add ATP, RNase inhibitor and T7 DNA Ligase just before ready to load into Round 1 barcoding plate 260 µL, GLR Buffer, 4x 20.8 µL, ATP, 100mM 52 µL, dNTPs, 25 mM each 13 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL) 6.76 µL, Enzymatics RNase In, (40U/uL) 10.4 µL, DTT, 1M 78 µL, T7 DNA Ligase (3000U/uL) 1.04 µL, Nuclease-free water

steps.title: Add 8 µL of nuclei to...
steps.instruction: Add 8 µL of nuclei to each well (row A – D) with a multichannel pipette.

steps.title: Aliquot 73 µL of GLR-A into...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 73 µL of GLR-A into 6 tube-strip on PCR cool rack and add 8.5 µL of GLR-A mix to each well with a multichannel pipette.

steps.title: Seal and quick spin the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and quick spin the plate at 160 g for 15 s, 4 ºC. Gently mix reaction mix, Round 1 barcoded oligos and nuclei 5 times (mixing volume 18 µL)

steps.title: Seal and quick spin the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and quick spin the plate at 160 g for 10 s, 4 ºC and incubate the plate on thermomixer at 25 ºC for 30 min, 300 rpm 00:30:00, AC oligo ligation

steps.title: Aliquot 20 µL of Maxima H...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 20 µL of Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase into 6 tubes of PCR strip tube 120 µL, Maxima H Minus RT (200 U/uL)

steps.title: Remove Round 1 barcoding plate from...
steps.instruction: Remove Round 1 barcoding plate from thermomixer, put on PCR cool rack, and add 2.2 µL of Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase to each well with a multichannel pipette. CRITICAL STEP: To get accurate volumes, set the speed of multichannel pipette to be very slow for aspirating and dispensing as the enzyme is very viscous

steps.title: Seal and quick spin the plate...
steps.instruction: Seal and quick spin the plate at 160 g for 15 s, 4 ºC to bring enzyme to the bottom of the well and mix by gently pipetting 5 times (mixing volume 18 µL). Seal and quick spin the plate at 160 g for 10 s, 4ºC

steps.title: Incubate the plate on thermocycler using the program
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate on thermocycler using the program: 50ºC for 10 min, 3 cycles of (8ºC for 12 s, 15ºC for 45 s, 20ºC for 45 s, 30ºC 30 s, 42ºC for 2 min, 50ºC for 3 min), 50ºC for 5 min.

steps.title: Before reverse transcription finishes, prepare 3...
steps.instruction: Before reverse transcription finishes, prepare 3 mL of 1x PBS + 0.1% BSA + RI 3000 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 40 µL, BSA, 7.5% wt/vol 4.5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 2.25 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: Remove the plate from thermocycler and...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from thermocycler and put on ice or PCR cool rack then pool all reactions into a chilled 25 mL reservoir and transfer pooled reaction into a chilled 5 mL DNA LoBind tube. CRITICAL STEP: Before pooling, pipette Round 1 barcoding plate 2 times to kick up nuclei from the bottom of the well and make sure that you transfer all reactions from the well by aspirating slowly and set pipette volume to 26 µL. This technique should be done for all pooling steps. Also keep reactions on ice all the time when handling samples or reaction mixes to prevent RNA degradation and preserve enzyme activity or temperature sensitive reagents like ATP.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.8 mL of 1x PBS + 0.1% (wt/vol) BSA + RI (2.5x volume) to rinse the basin and transfer buffer to the tube

steps.title: Add 19
steps.instruction: Add 19.5 µL of 10% (vol/vol) Triton X-100 (final concentration of Triton X-100 is 0.05% (vol/vol)) and mix by inverting the tube 5 times before centrifuge at 900 g for 8 min, 4 ºC 19.5 µL, Triton X-100, 10% vol/vol

steps.title: Thaw Round 2 barcoded oligos working...
steps.instruction: Thaw Round 2 barcoded oligos working plate on ice and spin the plate at 200 g, for 1 min, 4 ºC and leave the plate on ice. Note: Can leave in 4C at the beginning of Day 1 to thaw.

steps.title: Prepare 1x Buffer 3.1 as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare 1x Buffer 3.1 as follows: 210 µL, NEBuffer 3.1, 10x 21 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40U/uL) 1890 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Prepare Ligation Mix as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare Ligation Mix as follows: 510 µL, T4 DNA Ligase Buffer, 10x 40.8 µL, Enzymatics RNase In (40U/uL) 12.75 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL) 51 µL, BSA (20 mg/mL) 127.5 µL, T4 DNA Ligase (400U/uL) 1338.8 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Remove supernatant as much as possible...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant as much as possible (~20 µL left) and resuspend nuclei with 2.02 mL of 1x Buffer 3.1 and add 2.04 mL of Ligation mix and mix by pipetting 10 times

steps.title: Transfer nuclei in ligation mix into...
steps.instruction: Transfer nuclei in ligation mix into 25 mL reservoir and add 40 µL of nuclei suspension into each well of Round 2 barcoding plate and mix gently by pipetting 5 times (mixing volume 45 µL)

steps.title: Seal the plate with sealing film...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with sealing film and quick spin the plate at 160 g for 10 s, 4 ºC

steps.title: Incubate the plate on thermomixer at...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate on thermomixer at 37 ºC for 30 min, 300 rpm 00:30:00, Round 2 barcoding

steps.title: Prepare Round 2 blocking solution as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare Round 2 blocking solution as follows: 47.52 µL, BC_0216, 1000 uM 300 µL, T4 DNA Ligase Buffer, 10x 852.5 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Remove Round 2 DNA barcoding plate...
steps.instruction: Remove Round 2 DNA barcoding plate from thermomixer and quick spin at 160 g for 10 s, 4 ºC. Add 10 µL of Round 2 blocking solution to each well with multichannel pipette and mix by pipetting gently 5 times (mixing volume 55 µL)

steps.title: Seal the plate with sealing film...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with sealing film and quick spin at 160 g for 10 s, 4ºC then incubate the plate on thermomixer at 37 ºC for 30 min, 300 rpm 00:30:00, Round 2 blocking

steps.title: Thaw Round 3 barcoded oligos working...
steps.instruction: Thaw Round 3 barcoded oligos working plate on ice, spin the plate on swinging bucket at 200 g for 1 min, 4 ºC and leave the plate on ice

steps.title: Place Round 2 DNA barcoding plate...
steps.instruction: Place Round 2 DNA barcoding plate on PCR cool rack and pool into 25 mL reservoir. Add 100 µL of T4 DNA Ligase (400 U/µL) into the basin with nuclei from Round 2 barcoding plate and mix well by gently pipetting 10 times and rock the basin from side-to-side 10 times 100 µL, T4 DNA Ligase, (400 U/uL)

steps.title: Add 50 µL of nuclei suspension...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of nuclei suspension to each well of Round 3 DNA barcoding plate and mix gently by pipetting 5 times (mixing volume 55 µL).

steps.title: Seal the plate with sealing film...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with sealing film and briefly spin the plate at 160 g for 10 s, 4ºC. Incubate the plate on thermomixer at 37 ºC for 30min, 300 rpm 00:30:00, Round 3 barcoding

steps.title: Prepare Round 3 blocking solution as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare Round 3 blocking solution as follows: 41.4 µL, BC_0066, 1000 uM 600 µL, EDTA, 500mM 1758.6 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Remove Round 3 DNA barcoding plate...
steps.instruction: Remove Round 3 DNA barcoding plate from thermomixer and add 20 µL of Round 3 blocking solution to each well and gently mix by pipetting 3 times (mixing volume 75 µL)

steps.title: Without incubation, pool the reaction into...
steps.instruction: Without incubation, pool the reaction into 25 mL reservoir placed on ice, transfer supernatant into 15 mL tube and centrifuge at 1,000 g for 8 min, 4ºC. OPTIONAL: EDTA in the reaction inhibits ligase activity, therefor there is no need to change the pipette tips when pooling Round 3 ligation reaction. Set pipetting volume 85 µL when pooling nuclei.

steps.title: In parallel, prepare chilled wash buffer as follows
steps.instruction: In parallel, prepare chilled wash buffer as follows. 4000 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 40 µL, Triton X-100, 10% vol/vol 10 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: Also in parallel, thaw 2X Lysis...
steps.instruction: Also in parallel, thaw 2X Lysis buffer at 37C for ~15 minutes if previously prepped. If not see step 6 for recipe.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and add 4 mL of wash buffer
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and add 4 mL of wash buffer. Pipette gently 5 times with p1000 pipette then centrifuge at 1,000 g for 8 min, 4 ºC

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant as much as...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant as much as possible and resuspend nuclei with 300 µL of 1x PBS + RI 500 µL, PBS, pH 7.4 5 µL, SUPERase In (20 U/uL) 2.5 µL, Enzymatics Rnase In (40 U/uL)

steps.title: Count nuclei concentration with cell counter...
steps.instruction: Count nuclei concentration with cell counter and aliquot nuclei at required number in each pool in 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube and adjust total volume of nuclei to 50 µL with 1x PBS + RI. CRITICAL STEP: To sequence all nuclei, recommend using ≤20,000 cells per pool by splitting 50 µL of nuclei suspension into 6 - 12 tubes per plate.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of 2x Lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of 2x Lysis buffer and 10 µL of 20 mg/mL Proteinase K to each pool, mix well by gently vortexing and brief spin the tube down at room temperature for 10 s to collect all nuclei suspension to the bottom of the tube and incubate on thermomixer at 55 ºC for 2 h, 350 rpm to lyse nuclei and reverse crosslink formaldehyde fixation. 02:00:00, Nuclei lysis

steps.title: Put nuclei lysate at -80 ºC...
steps.instruction: Put nuclei lysate at -80 ºC to inactivate Proteinase K before continue to day 2 experiment. CRITICAL STEP: Lysis buffer tends to precipitate at room temperature or low temperature. Redissolve by incubating the tube at 37 ºC until it completely dissolves before adding to nuclei suspension. PAUSE POINT: Nuclear or cell lysate can be store at -80 ºC for up to 6 months before continue to day 2 experiment.

steps.title: Prepare 3
steps.instruction: Prepare 3.5 mL of 1x B&W-T + RI for bead washing: 3500 µL, B&W-T, 1x 5 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: Vortex the bottle of Dynabeads MyOne...
steps.instruction: Vortex the bottle of Dynabeads MyOne C1 thoroughly and aliquot required volume (44 µL/pool * number of pool) into 1.5 mL tube. Add 800 µL of 1x B&W-T + RI, mix by vortexing and pulse spin on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 3 s 44 µL, MyOne C1 beads

steps.title: Place the tubes onto the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes onto the magnetic rack until liquid is clear; Remove supernatant with p1000 pipette

steps.title: Resuspend the beads with 800 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads with 800 µL of 1x B&W-T + RI, vortex and pulse spin on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 3 s

steps.title: Place the tubes onto the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes onto the magnetic rack until solution is clear and remove supernatant with p1000 pipette

steps.title: Repeat washes two more times (total of 3 washes)
steps.instruction: Repeat washes two more times (total of 3 washes)

steps.title: Resuspend the beads with 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads with 100 µL of 2x B&W + RI per pool: 100 µL, 2x B&W-T 2 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: During bead preparation, take the tubes...
steps.instruction: During bead preparation, take the tubes of lysate out of -80 ºC and place onto thermomixer set at 55 ºC for 2 min until lysate is completely thawed

steps.title: Add 5 µL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of 0.1 M PMSF (from -20 ºC) to each tube, pulse vortex for 10 s and pulse spin on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 5 s and incubate at room temperature for 10 min with no shaking 5 µL, PMSF, 100 mM 00:10:00

steps.title: Add 100 µL of streptavidin beads...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of streptavidin beads in 2x B&W + RI to each tube of lysate (no pipetting required) then agitate the tubes on mixer at room temperature for 1 h, 1,200 rpm. CRITICAL STEP: We recommend to use orbital shaker for microtubes. The speed of mixer can be adjusted as long as the beads do not settle on the bottom of the tube 01:00:00, Binding to beads

steps.title: Pulse spin the tubes on mini...
steps.instruction: Pulse spin the tubes on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 5 s, place onto magnetic rack until solution is clear and remove supernatant with p200 pipette. CRITICAL STEP: Every time before placing the tube back to the magnet, quick spin the tubes on mini centrifuge for 3-5 s to collect all supernatant/lysate/buffer and beads to the bottom of the tube. Use p200 pipette to remove supernatant to avoid disturbing the beads and prevent bead loss.

steps.title: Prepare 1 mL of 1x B&W-T...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 mL of 1x B&W-T + RI (750 uL per lysate) for bead washing as follows: 1000 µL, B&W-T, 1x 1.4 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: Add 250 µL of 1 x...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL of 1 x B&W-T + RI and agitate the bead at room temperature for 5 min, 1,500 rpm to wash the beads 00:05:00, wash with 1x B&W-T + RI

steps.title: Place the tube onto magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Place the tube onto magnetic rack until solution is clear and remove supernatant. Continue to template switching oligo blocking on AC DNA immediately.

steps.title: During working on Steps 109-110, prepare...
steps.instruction: During working on Steps 109-110, prepare Nextera adapter 1 blocking solution per pool: 250 µL, SSC, 6x 2.5 µL, Nextera adapter 1 blocker, 100 uM 1 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: Add 250 µL of 6x SSC...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL of 6x SSC to each tube without bead suspension and wait until the supernatant is clear then remove supernatant with p200 pipette 250 µL, SSC, 6x

steps.title: Add 250 µL of Nextera adapter...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL of Nextera adapter 1 blocking solution to each tube and agitate the tubes on mixer at room temperature for 1 min, 1,500 rpm then reduce the speed to 500 rpm for 14 min. CRITICAL STEP: Agitate the tubes at high speed 1,500 rpm for 1 min to make sure the beads are resuspended well, then shake gently at 500 rpm for 14 min to allow hybridization of Nextera adapter 1 blocker and Nextera adpter 1 on AC DNA and make sure that the beads do not settle. 00:15:00, incubate with Nextera Blocking Solution

steps.title: Place the tube onto magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Place the tube onto magnetic rack until solution is clear and remove supernatant with p200 pipette.

steps.title: Wash the beads twice with 1x...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads twice with 1x B&W-T + RI as described above at room temperature for 5 min each round. 00:05:00, wash with 1x B&W-T + RI 00:05:00, wash #2 with 1x B&W-T + RI

steps.title: In parallel, prepare Tris-T + RI...
steps.instruction: In parallel, prepare Tris-T + RI during second bead wash 250 µL, Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 10 mM 2.5 µL, Tween 20, 10% vol/vol 0.63 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL)

steps.title: Wash the beads with 250 µL...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads with 250 µL of Tris-T + RI (5 min) the same way as 1x B&W-T + RI wash (at room temperature for 5 min, 1,500 rpm). In parallel, prepare GLR-B mix. CRITICAL STEP: If GLR-B mix is not ready, leave the beads in the tube with Tris-T + RI on ice until GLR-B mix is ready 00:05:00, wash with Tris-T + RI

steps.title: During washing, prepare GLR-B mix as follows
steps.instruction: During washing, prepare GLR-B mix as follows: 50 µL, GLR buffer, 4x 4 µL, ATP, 100mM 20 µL, Ficoll PM 400, 20% wt/vol 10 µL, dNTPs, 25 mM each 5 µL, SUPERase In (20U/uL) 2 µL, DTT , 1M 12.5 µL, Hemo Klentag 2.5 µL, T7 DNA Ligase (3000U/uL) 89 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Place the tubes onto magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes onto magnetic rack until solution is clear, remove supernatant with p200 pipette and add 250 µL of nuclease-free water to each tube without bead suspension 250 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Remove water and resuspend the beads...
steps.instruction: Remove water and resuspend the beads with 195 µL of GLR-B mix by gently vortexing and quick spin the tubes on mini centrifuge for 3 s

steps.title: Rotate the tubes in incubator at...
steps.instruction: Rotate the tubes in incubator at 37 ºC with slow speed for 15 min to allow for gap filling on AC DNA and ligate Nextera adapter 1 blocker to the AC DNA. OPTIONAL: For any step required rotator, thermomixer can be alternatively used as long as the tube is shaken gently and the beads do not settle. 00:15:00, Gap filling and ligation

steps.title: Remove the tubes from incubator and...
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from incubator and add each tube with 5 µL of 100 µM TSO and 5 µL of Maxima H Minus reverse transcriptase and mix well by gently vortexing. 5 µL, TSO oligo, 100 uM 5 µL, Maxima H Minus RT, (200 U/uL)

steps.title: Continue to incubate at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Continue to incubate at room temperature for 30 min with slow rotation. 00:30:00, complete reverse transcription

steps.title: Incubate at 42 ºC for 90 min with slow rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate at 42 ºC for 90 min with slow rotation. 01:30:00, complete reverse transcription

steps.title: PAUSE POINT
steps.instruction: PAUSE POINT: The beads can be stored in Tris-T buffer at 4 ºC overnight before continuing to 1st PCR (Remove supernatant and replace with Tris-T buffer). However, we recommend to continue to the 1st PCR immediately if possible.

steps.title: Before finishing 42C incubation, set up...
steps.instruction: Before finishing 42C incubation, set up the first PCR mix to amplify both cDNA and accessible chromatin (AC) DNA and prepare PCR strip tubes with individual hinged cap (4 tubes per pool): 110 µL, KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix, 2x 8.8 µL, BC_0108, 10 uM 17.6 µL, BC_0062, 10 uM 8.8 µL, BC_0082, 10 uM 74.8 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: After finishing incubation at 42 ºC...
steps.instruction: After finishing incubation at 42 ºC for 90 min, place the tubes onto the magnetic rack until liquid is clear and remove supernatant with p200 pipette

steps.title: Add 250 µL of nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL of nuclease-free water to each tube without bead resuspension. Once liquid is clear, remove supernatant. 250 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Resuspend the beads with 220 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads with 220 µL of first PCR mix, quick spin and aliquot 55 µL of bead suspension in PCR mix to each of 4 PCR strip tubes. Transfer all the leftover beads to 4 PCR tubes equally. CRITICAL STEP: Transfer the beads in PCR mix directly to the bottom of the tube, so there is no need to spin the tube before placing on thermocycler. Try to transfer PCR mix with the beads into PCR strip tubes as quick as possible to minimize bead settling before the reaction starts.

steps.title: Place the tubes on thermocycler and run following program
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on thermocycler and run following program: 95 ºC for 3 min, 9 cycles of (98 ºC for 20 s, 58 ºC for 45 s, 72 ºC for 3 min), 72 ºC for 5 min, 4 ºC hold.

steps.title: PAUSE POINT PCR reaction can be...
steps.instruction: PAUSE POINT PCR reaction can be stored at -20 ºC for a month or 4 ºC for a week.

steps.title: Place strip tubes onto 0
steps.instruction: Place strip tubes onto 0.2 mL magnetic rack until supernatant is clear and pool 1st round PCR products from 4 strip tubes of the same pool into 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube

steps.title: Vortex the tube and quick spin...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube and quick spin on mini centrifuge for 3 s before splitting PCR products into two parts (100 µL each), “AC” for chromatin accessibility (AC) library preparation and “R” for RNA library preparation. Note: AC and R in this step are the same PCR products but will be bead size-selected at different bead volume ratio.

steps.title: Perform one round of 1
steps.instruction: Perform one round of 1.2x KAPA Pure Beads purification following manufacturer’s instructions by using 120 µL of KAPA Pure Beads with 100 µL of PCR products and elute with 40 µL of nuclease-free water 1. Binding 120 µL, KAPA Pure Beads 00:08:00 2. Washing 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 3. 2nd wash 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 4. Drying 00:01:00 37 °C 5. Elution 40 µL, Nuclease free water 00:10:00 37 °C

steps.title: Transfer eluent into new PCR strip tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer eluent into new PCR strip tubes. The resultant is called AC-A.

steps.title: Use 2 µL of AC-A product...
steps.instruction: Use 2 µL of AC-A product to determine DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit following manufacturer’s instruction

steps.title: Verify ~10 ng of AC-A in...
steps.instruction: Verify ~10 ng of AC-A in 6% TBE gel and run in 1x TBE buffer at 250 volts for 23 min with 0.5 µL of Low DNA Mass Ladder as reference

steps.title: In PCR strip tubes, use 5...
steps.instruction: In PCR strip tubes, use 5 ng of AC-A as template for enrichment of AC DNA over cDNA and adjust volume of template to 10 µL with nuclease-free water and quick spin down the tubes. CRITICAL STEP: If the concentration of PCR product is higher than 5 ng/µL, it tends to have high error to pipette the volume smaller than 1 µL. We recommend to aliquot AC-A, dilute into 0.5 ng/µL in total 30-50 µL and use 10 µL as template for AC 2nd PCR. 10 µL, AC-A DNA (total 5 ng)

steps.title: Prepare PCR mix as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR mix as follows: 25 µL, KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix, 2x 2.5 µL, SPLiT_N701, 10 uM 2.5 µL, EvaGreen, 20x 7.5 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Add 37
steps.instruction: Add 37.5 µL of PCR mix and 2.5 µL of 10 µM Ad1_N50X (attached tables) into the tube with AC-A template 2.5 µL, Ad1_N5XX, 10 uM

steps.title: Mix by gently vortexing, quick spin...
steps.instruction: Mix by gently vortexing, quick spin PCR tubes on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 3 s and run qPCR on real-time PCR machine: 95 ºC for 3 min, 12 (or fewer) cycles of (98 ºC for 20 s, 58 ºC for 45 s, 72 ºC for 1 min), 72 ºC for 5 min, 4 ºC hold.

steps.title: PAUSE POINT
steps.instruction: PAUSE POINT: PCR reaction can be stored at -20 ºC for a month or 4 ºC for a week.

steps.title: Purify PCR product with DNA Clean...
steps.instruction: Purify PCR product with DNA Clean & Concentrator following manufacturer's instructions and elute with 40 µL of DNA Elution Buffer. Resultant is called AC-B

steps.title: Use 2 µL of AC-B to...
steps.instruction: Use 2 µL of AC-B to determine DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit following manufacturing’s instruction

steps.title: Verify ~10 ng of AC-B in...
steps.instruction: Verify ~10 ng of AC-B in 6% TBE gel and run in 1x TBE buffer at 250 volts for 23 min with 0.5 µL of Low DNA Mass Ladder as a reference in separate lane. CRITICAL STEP: We expect to see nucleosome pattern with larger size (~125 bp larger) compare to standard ATAC-seq due to the presence of cell barcodes and linker sequences

steps.title: Pool equimolar ratio of AC-B libraries...
steps.instruction: Pool equimolar ratio of AC-B libraries (~200-250 ng/pool) and perform PAGE size-selection at the size between 300-1,000 bp.

steps.title: Use 2 µL of AC sequencing...
steps.instruction: Use 2 µL of AC sequencing libraries to determine for DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay

steps.title: Verify ~5 -10 ng of AC...
steps.instruction: Verify ~5 -10 ng of AC sequencing libraries in 6% TBE gel and run in 1x TBE buffer at 250 volts for 23 min or by TapeStation

steps.title: Validate AC sequencing libraries with MiSeq...
steps.instruction: Validate AC sequencing libraries with MiSeq sequencing using v2 reagent kit by loading at 20 pm based on Qubit dsDNA HS quantification with at least 5% PhiX spike in following Illumina loading guide. The sequencing configuration is 75 cycles for read 1, 94 cycles for index 1, 8 cycles for index 2, and 75 cycles for read 2. CRITICAL STEP: For MiSeq sequencing, quantification of sequencing library concentration by Qubit and determination of average library size is sufficient. For high-throughput sequencing, we recommend to run sequencing libraries on Bioanalyzer, TapeStation or equivalent instrument that can determine accurate average library size and contamination of adapter dimers peaks at approximately 125-175 bp. We recommend to run qPCR to quantify sequencing library concentration using the average library size derived from TapeStation. For a two-channel sequencing system such as NovaSeq, we recommend to spike-in with minimum of 10% PhiX or consult sequencing core.

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R1"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R1" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing read 1 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2_Read1, 100 uM 3 µL, PhiX_Read1, 100 uM 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R2"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R2" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing index 1 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2-AC_BCread, 100 uM 3 µL, PhiX_Read1, 100 uM 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R4"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-AC R4" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing read 2 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2-AC_Read2, 100 uM 3 µL, PhiX_Read2, 100 uM 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1

steps.title: Perform 1 round of 0
steps.instruction: Perform 1 round of 0.8x KAPA Pure Beads purification following manufacturer’s instructions by mixing 100 µL of 1st PCR product + with 80 µL of KAPA Pure Beads and elute with 40 µL of nuclease-free water. Resultant is called R-A. 1. Binding 80 µL, KAPA Pure Beads 00:08:00 2. Washing 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 3. 2nd wash 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 4. Drying 00:01:00 37 °C 5. Elution 40 µL, Nuclease free water 00:10:00

steps.title: Use 2 µL of R-A to...
steps.instruction: Use 2 µL of R-A to determine DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay following manufacturer’s instructions

steps.title: Verify 5-10 ng of R-A in...
steps.instruction: Verify 5-10 ng of R-A in 6% TBE gel and run in 1x TBE buffer at 250 volts for 23 min with 0.5 µL of Low DNA Mass Ladder as a reference

steps.title: In PCR strip tubes, use 5...
steps.instruction: In PCR strip tubes, use 5 ng of R-A as template for enrichment of cDNA over AC DNA and adjust the volume of template to 10 µL with nuclease-free water and quick spin down the tubes. CRITICAL STEP: If the concentration of PCR product is higher than 5 ng/µL, it tends to have high error to pipette the volume smaller than 1 µL. We recommend to aliquot R-A, dilute into 0.5 ng/µL in total 30-50 µL and use 10 µL as template for RNA 2nd PCR

steps.title: Prepare PCR mix for second round...
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR mix for second round amplification of cDNA as follows: 25 µL, KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix, 2x 2 µL, BC_0108, 10 uM 2 µL, BC_0062, 10 uM 2.5 µL, EvaGreen, 20x 8.5 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Add 40 µL of PCR mix...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL of PCR mix to each tube, gently vortex and quick spin PCR tubes on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 3 s

steps.title: Run the reactions on real-time PCR using following program
steps.instruction: Run the reactions on real-time PCR using following program: 95 ºC for 3 min, 12 (or fewer) cycles of (98 ºC for 20 s, 67 ºC for 45 s, 72 ºC for 3 min), 72 ºC for 5 min, 4 ºC hold.

steps.title: PAUSE POINT PCR reaction can be...
steps.instruction: PAUSE POINT PCR reaction can be stored at -20 ºC for a month or 4 ºC for a week.

steps.title: Purify PCR products with 1 round of 0
steps.instruction: Purify PCR products with 1 round of 0.8x KAPA Pure Beads and elute with 40 µL of nuclease-free water. The resultant purified DNA is called R-B 1. Binding 40 µL, KAPA Pure Beads 00:08:00 2. Washing 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 3. 2nd wash 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 4. Drying 00:01:00 37 °C 5. Elution 40 µL, Nuclease free water 00:10:00 37 °C

steps.title: Determine DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA...
steps.instruction: Determine DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay using 2 µL of R-B

steps.title: Verify ~5-10 ng of R-B in...
steps.instruction: Verify ~5-10 ng of R-B in 6% TBE gel by running at 250 volts for 23 min with 0.5 µL of Low DNA Mass Ladder as a reference. CRITICAL STEP: If the smear of R-B is between 375 bp and above, continue to cDNA tagmentation. If there is strong smear smaller than 375 bp, repeat another round of 0.8x KAKA Pure Beads and elute with 40 µL of nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Mix ME and Nextera Adapter 1...
steps.instruction: Mix ME and Nextera Adapter 1 (Ad1) oligos 1:1 for final 50 uM each. Briefly vortex and quick spin the tube. Anneal transposons on thermocycler using following program: 95 ºC 5 min, slowly cool down to 65 ºC (0.1 ºC/s), 65 ºC 5 min, slowly cool down to 4 ºC (0.1 ºC/s) and hold at 4 ºC.

steps.title: Load Tn5 (In-house, 11
steps.instruction: Load Tn5 (In-house, 11.32 uM) 20 µL, Tn5, (In-house, 11.32 uM) 6.8 µL, 50 uM, Nextera Adapter 1/ME

steps.title: Incubate 1 hour at room temperate with gentle shaking
steps.instruction: Incubate 1 hour at room temperate with gentle shaking. 01:00:00

steps.title: Prepare Tagmentation mix as follows in 1
steps.instruction: Prepare Tagmentation mix as follows in 1.5 mL tube and aliquot 18 µL per pool into 0.2 mL PCR strip tubes with hinged cap. 2 µL, Tango Buffer, 10X 2 µL, DMF, 100% 12 µL, Nuclease free water 2 µL, Loaded Tn5, 8.44 uM

steps.title: Transfer 2 µL of diluted cDNA...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 µL of diluted cDNA (total 10-20 ng) with multichannel pipette into tagmentation mix, mix by pipetting 10 times, gently vortex and quick spin on mini centrifuge at room temperature for 5 s

steps.title: Place the tubes on thermocycler that...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on thermocycler that set at 55 ºC for 7 min with the heated lid 00:07:00

steps.title: Remove the tube from thermocycler and...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from thermocycler and stop reaction by adding 5 µL of 0.2 % (wt/vol) SDS, mix by pipetting 5 times, gently spin down the tubes on mini centrifuge for 5 s and incubate at room temperature for 5 min 5 µL, 0.2% SDS 00:05:00

steps.title: Set up Tagmentation PCR mix during...
steps.instruction: Set up Tagmentation PCR mix during incubation 25 µL, KAPA Hifi Hotstart ReadyMix, 2X 2.5 µL, BC_0118, 10 uM 2.5 µL, EvaGreen, 20X 7.5 µL, Nuclease free water

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 SPLiT_N7XX (attached tables) primers to 10 uL tagmented cDNA, then 37.5 of Tagmentation PCR mix respectively, mix well and gently spin down the tube on mini centrifuge for 3 s 2.5 µL, SPLiT_N7XX, 10 uM 10 µL, tagmented cDNA 37.5 µL, Tagmentation PCR Mix

steps.title: Run PCR on thermocycler using program follows
steps.instruction: Run PCR on thermocycler using program follows: 72 ºC for 3 min, 98 ºC for 30 s, 9-12 cycles of (98 ºC for 30 s, 63 ºC for 30 s, 72 ºC for 45 s), 72 ºC for 3 min, 4 ºC hold. Terminate the reaction when it reaches mid-exponential growth.

steps.title: Purify PCR products at least two rounds of 0
steps.instruction: Purify PCR products at least two rounds of 0.7x KAPA Pure beads to make sure that no adapter dimers are leftover in the sequencing libraries. Elute the last round of bead purified RNA libraries with 30 µL of nuclease-free water. CRITICAL STEP: Adapter dimers can cluster more efficiently than regular sequencing libraries and is more sensitive on the patterned flow cell such as NovaSeq S4. This can lead to the reduction of sequencing output and sequencing quality 1. Binding 35 µL, KAPA Pure Beads 00:08:00 2. Washing 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 3. 2nd wash 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 4. Drying 00:01:00 37 °C 5. Elution 50 µL, Nuclease free water 00:10:00 37 °C 6. Binding 35 µL, KAPA Pure Beads 00:08:00 7. Washing 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 8. 2nd wash 180 µL, Ethanol, 100% 00:00:30 9. Drying 00:01:00 37 °C 10. Elution 30 µL, Nuclease free water 00:10:00 37 °C

steps.title: Use 1 µL of RNA sequencing...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µL of RNA sequencing libraries to determine for DNA concentration with Qubit dsDNA HS assay

steps.title: Verify ~ 5 ng of RNA...
steps.instruction: Verify ~ 5 ng of RNA libraries in 6% TBE gel at 250 volts for 23 min with 0.5 µL of Low DNA Mass Ladder as a reference

steps.title: Validate sequencing libraries with MiSeq sequencing...
steps.instruction: Validate sequencing libraries with MiSeq sequencing using v3 reagent kit by loading at 22 pm based on Qubit dsDNA HS quantification with at least 5% PhiX spike. The sequencing configuration is 70 cycles for read 1, 6 cycles for index, and 102 cycles for read 2.a CRITICAL STEP: For MiSeq sequencing, quantification of sequencing library concentration by Qubit and determination of average library size is sufficient. For high-throughput sequencing, we recommend to run sequencing libraries on Bioanalyzer, TapeStation or equivalent instrument that can determine accurate average library size and contamination of adapter dimers peaks at approximately 125-175 bp. We recommend running qPCR to quantify sequencing library concentration using the average library size derived from TapeStation. For a two-channel sequencing system such as NovaSeq, we recommend spike-in with a minimum of 10% PhiX or consult sequencing core.

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R R1"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R R1" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing read 1 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2_Read1, 100 uM 3 µL, PhiX_Read1, 100 uM 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R Index1"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R Index1" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing index 1 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2-R_Index1 3 µL, PhiX_Read1 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1

steps.title: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R R2"...
steps.instruction: Mix 1:1 ratio of "SN2-R R2" sequencing primer pairs for sequencing read 2 on Illumina workflow A (HiSeq 2500, MiSeq, NovaSeq 6000 platforms). 3 µL, SNARE2-R_Read2 3 µL, PhiX_Read2 594 µL, Hybridization buffer, HT1
